lines.en_lit: it is possible with non-sacred obvious No, necessary on Shabbat,
to Shabbat also obvious, moving, by rabbinic With what are we dealing here? an imperforate container, by rabbinic
One Sage, holds that untithed produce is more severe, and one Sage, holds that teruma is more severe.

Let us say is tanna’im, as it was taught one whom a snake bit one calls a doctor for him from place to another; and one tears a chicken apart for him and one harvests leeks and feeds him and one need not separate tithes; the statement of Rabbi Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says: He should not eat it unless it has been tithed.
Let us say Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon. And not Rabbi

Even if you say Rabbi Rabbi is saying only there, with regard to tithe from vegetables, which is rabbinic However, tithe of grains, untithed produce by Torah even Rabbi concedes Because if it is permitted for him an imperforate container, come to eat a perforated pot,

The Sages taught one who is seized with bulmos, one feeds him honey and all types of sweet as the honey and all types of sweet restore the sight of his eyes. And although there is no proof for the matter, there is an allusion to the matter. “See, I pray you, how my eyes are brightened because I tasted a little of this honey”
And why Although there is no proof for the matter, There, not seized with bulmos,

Abaye said: They taught only after eating but before eating it whets as it is written: “And they found an Egyptian man in the field, and brought him to David, and they gave him bread and he ate, and they gave him water to drink; and they gave him a piece of a cake of figs, and two clusters of raisins, and he ate, and his spirit was restored; for he had eaten no bread nor drunk any water for three days and three nights”

Rav Naḥman said Shmuel said: one who is seized with bulmos, one feeds him a sheep’s tail with honey, Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, said: Also, fine wheat flour with honey Rav Pappa said: Even barley flour with honey Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Once I was seized with bulmos and I ran to the east side of a fig tree And I fulfilled “Wisdom preserves the lives of those who have it” As Rav Yosef taught: One who wishes to taste the flavor of the fig should turn to the east, as it is stated: “And for the precious things of the sun’s fruits”

Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Yosei were walking on the road Rabbi Yehuda was seized with bulmos. He overpowered shepherd and ate the bread Rabbi Yosei said to him: You have robbed that shepherd. When they reached the city, Rabbi Yosei was seized with bulmos, surrounded him with jugs [lagei] and plates Rabbi Yehuda said to him I robbed the shepherd, but you have robbed the entire city.

And furthermore, Rabbi Meir and Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Yosei were walking on the road Rabbi Meir would analyze names Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Yosei were not analyze names. When they came to a certain place, they looked for lodging were given They said What is your name? He said to them: Kidor. said learn from this that he is a wicked person, as it is stated: “For they are a generation [ki dor] of upheavals” Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Yosei entrusted their purses to him. Rabbi Meir did not entrust his purse to him went and placed it at the grave of the innkeeper’s father.

appeared in a dream Go take the purse placed at the head of that man, The following day, he said This appeared to me in my dream. They said to him: Dreams during twilight have no substance Rabbi Meir went and guarded all day and took it.

The next day, said to Give us our purses. He said to them: These matters never occurred; Rabbi Meir said to them: Why didn’t you analyze his name They said to him: Why didn’t the Master tell us? He said to them: I said one should be suspicious, but have I said should be established as
They dragged and brought him to a store they saw lentils on his mustache, They went and gave this sign to his wife. And they took their purses He went and killed his wife
This is as we learned the first washing, they fed pork. the final washing, to kill the person.

And in the end, they would analyze names. When they came to a house of named Bala, they did not enter. They said: Conclude from here that he is wicked, as it is written: “I said of her who was worn out [bala] by adulteries” as “After I am grown old [beloti] shall I have pleasure?” aged through adulteries.

one whom a mad dog bit, The Sages taught Five signs were said about a mad dog: Its mouth is open; and its saliva drips; and its ears are floppy its tail rests on its legs; and it walks on the edges of roads. And some say it also barks and its voice is not heard. From where did Rav said: Witches play with it And Shmuel said: An evil spirit rests upon it.
What is between these There is between them
